intro: Simsia benziorum, also known as the Simsia bush, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, Simsia benziorum has been used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and infection. The bioactive constituents of Simsia benziorum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Simsia benziorum is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture, as well as in the form of a powder or capsule, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
